app.name: ChatFlick by coreXmanzoid
app.description: A Flask-based social microblogging web application with email and Google authentication, follower relationships, post creation and engagement, comments, notifications, profile management, settings navigation, and a Groq-powered AI assistant.

authentication.description: Handles account registration, credential-based login, Google OAuth login, password reset, logout, and email-based identity flows.
user_actions: Sign up with name, username, phone, email, birth date, and password
Check username availability during signup
Log in with username or email plus password
Log in with Google OAuth
Request a password reset link
Reset password using a tokenized link
Log out from the current session
system_responses: Creates users with hashed passwords and default UNVERIFIED status
Rejects duplicate emails and duplicate usernames
Requires CAPTCHA validation for signup, login, and password reset request flows
Creates authenticated sessions after successful credential or Google login
Returns a generic success message for password reset requests whether the email exists or not
Redirects users to landing, login, or home pages depending on the flow
edge_cases: Password reset and email verification tokens expire after 20 minutes
Google login fails if Google user info does not contain an email
Google-created accounts are stored with status 'Verified' instead of exact 'VERIFIED', which can conflict with strict verification checks
Signup username availability returns status 'abondonded' when the username exists
Logout behavior changes by route state value rather than by a dedicated session policy

membership_and_verification.description: Manages user status tiers and feature gating across the application, including email verification and future premium or pro access.
user_actions: Request a verification email after login
Verify email using a signed token link
Access verified-only features such as follow, comment, mention notification, and profile image upload
View premium or verified upgrade prompts in the UI
system_responses: Transitions verified users from UNVERIFIED to VERIFIED
Shows an email verification overlay on the home screen for unverified users
Blocks verified-only routes with a 403 response when the status is not exactly VERIFIED
Treats PRO users as verified on the home page experience
edge_cases: The verification guard only accepts exact status 'VERIFIED' and rejects 'PRO' and 'Verified' in protected routes
Some routes are protected only by login and not by verification status
A pro_user decorator exists but is not actively applied to routes
Premium and verified upgrade UI exists, but there is no implemented backend upgrade or billing flow

account_discovery.description: Supports discovery of accounts through random suggestions, follower/following views, and username-based search.
user_actions: Load random account suggestions
Search accounts by username substring
View the accounts a user follows
View the followers of a user
system_responses: Returns up to 10 random user accounts for discovery
Returns a target user's follower or following lists based on route state
Searches usernames using partial matching and ranks results by follower count
Returns current user's following IDs so the UI can mark already-followed accounts
edge_cases: Search only targets usernames, not names or other profile fields
If search results exceed 10, a random sample of 10 is returned
Follower and following exploration depends on a valid target user ID
Route behavior is state-string driven and falls back to search logic for unknown non-special states

social_graph.description: Manages follow and unfollow relationships between users and exposes follower counts for profile and discovery views.
user_actions: Follow another user
Unfollow another user
View follower and following counts on profiles
system_responses: Creates a Follow relationship when following
Deletes the Follow relationship when unfollowing
Returns updated follower counts and current user identity data after follow state changes
edge_cases: Following and unfollowing require the user to be verified
There is no explicit prevention for self-following
There is no explicit prevention for duplicate follow rows
Unfollowing a non-existent relationship silently succeeds without error

posting.description: Handles post creation, editing, deletion, hashtag extraction, mention parsing, and author ownership checks.
user_actions: Create a new text post
Edit a recent post
Delete a recent post
Mention other users inside a post
system_responses: Rejects empty post content
Rejects post creation when the submitted user_id does not match the logged-in user
Extracts hashtags into a separate field and removes hashtag tokens from stored post content
Parses @mentions into structured user references
Allows edit and delete only for the post owner within a 3-minute window
Returns the created post ID and author username after successful creation
edge_cases: Backend does not enforce the frontend 180-character post limit
Editing a post does not re-parse hashtags or mentions
Post modification failure returns generic status messages instead of consistent HTTP error codes
Missing posts during edit or delete return a non-standard error payload

feed.description: Builds post collections for the home timeline, profile timeline, following feed, liked posts, reposted posts, and random discovery feed.
user_actions: Load the default random home feed
Load posts from followed accounts
Load posts created by a specific profile
Load posts previously liked by a user
Load posts previously reposted by a user
system_responses: Returns profile posts ordered by newest first
Returns followed-account posts ordered by newest first and limited to 10
Returns liked posts using the user's liked_posts JSON list
Returns reposted posts using the user's reposted_posts JSON list
Returns random posts for discovery when no specialized feed is requested
edge_cases: Following feed returns an empty list when the user follows nobody
Liked and reposted feeds return empty when the tracking arrays are empty
Random feed is not personalized
Reposted feed returns both posts and the reposting username for template rendering

engagement.description: Handles likes, reposts, shares, and comment likes as lightweight engagement actions on content.
user_actions: Like or unlike a post
Repost or undo repost on a post
Share or unshare a post
Like or unlike a comment
system_responses: Increments or decrements post like counts and tracks user-liked post IDs
Increments or decrements repost counts and tracks user-reposted post IDs
Increments or decrements share counts without per-user uniqueness tracking
Increments or decrements comment like counts
edge_cases: Post likes and reposts are unique per user because they are tracked in user JSON arrays
Comment likes are not uniquely tracked per user and can be repeatedly toggled without historical ownership checks
Counters are floored at zero on decrement operations
Missing posts or comments are not defensively handled before mutation, which can lead to runtime errors
Self-like, self-repost, and self-share are not blocked at the backend

comments.description: Supports threaded post discussion through comment retrieval and comment creation with mention parsing.
user_actions: Open a post's comment thread
Add a comment to a post
Mention other users inside a comment
system_responses: Returns the target post and up to 15 comments sorted by like count
Creates a new comment linked to the current user and target post
Parses mentions from comment content into structured user references
Increments the parent post's comment counter when a comment is added
edge_cases: Adding comments requires the user to be verified
Backend does not reject empty comment content before persistence
Comment retrieval is capped to the first 15 sorted results with no pagination
Invalid post handling is weak during comment creation and depends on database integrity behavior

notifications.description: Provides in-app and push notifications for follower broadcasts, single-recipient events, mention alerts, unread tracking, and token storage.
user_actions: Save a Firebase push token
Open the notifications inbox
Mark all notifications as read
Check unread notification count
Send follower-broadcast notifications
Send single-recipient notifications
Send mention notifications for posts and comments
system_responses: Stores notification records with sender, recipient, title, message, type, identifier, and read state
Returns the 15 most recent notifications for the current user
Marks all notifications for a user as read
Returns unread notification counts
Deduplicates generic notifications by type, identifier, and recipient via update_or_create
Attempts Firebase push delivery when requested and when configuration and recipient tokens are available
Skips self-notifications in single-recipient notification flow
edge_cases: Mention notifications do not use deduplication and can create repeated entries
Unread-count lookup does not verify that the requested user ID matches the logged-in user
Push delivery silently fails when Firebase is not initialized or the recipient lacks an FCM token
Mark-as-read enforces ownership, but other notification routes rely on partial sender checks
Follower-broadcast notifications exclude the sender from receiving their own broadcast

profile.description: Displays user profiles, exposes follower and following counts, and supports profile picture uploads through Cloudinary.
user_actions: Open a user profile
Open the new post composer via profile route with ID 0
Upload a new profile picture for the current account
Inspect follower and following counts
Switch between profile post tabs in the UI
system_responses: Returns profile details along with whether the current user follows the target profile
Returns the new post template when profile ID is 0
Uploads the selected profile image to Cloudinary with overwrite and resize transformations
Persists the uploaded image URL to the user record
edge_cases: Profile image upload requires login, verified status, file presence, and ownership match
Uploading with no file or an empty filename returns an error
Uploading for a non-existent user returns a not-found error
Only the image URL is editable; broader profile editing is not implemented in the backend

settings.description: Provides section-based settings page navigation for account and support views without backend persistence for preference updates.
user_actions: Open the settings page
Switch between settings sections such as profile-info, account-info, change-password, privacy-setting, notifications-setting, support, and delete-account
system_responses: Redirects the base settings route to the profile-info section
Renders the settings template when a valid section is requested
Redirects invalid sections back to the default profile-info section
edge_cases: There are no backend endpoints for saving settings changes
Delete-account exists as a settings section label but has no implemented deletion logic
Settings is currently a navigation shell rather than a full account management module

ai_assistant.description: Exposes two chat endpoints powered by Groq: an authenticated assistant with short per-user memory and a public stateless chat endpoint.
user_actions: Open the Manzoid-AI page after login
Send an authenticated AI message with user_id and message
Send a public AI message to the stateless API endpoint
system_responses: Rejects missing or empty messages
Limits authenticated chat messages to 130 characters
Limits public chat messages to 100 characters
Uses a short in-memory conversation history of the last 2 exchanges for authenticated chat
Returns generated replies from the Groq model
Returns a 503-style error response when the AI service is unavailable
edge_cases: Authenticated AI route requires user_id in the payload but does not verify that it matches current_user.id
Conversation history is stored in process memory and resets on app restart
Verified-user and pro-user gating are present in codebase concepts but not actively enforced on AI routes
AI availability depends on external API configuration and network connectivity
